brief_justification: Correctly identifies (C) and derives ~10,000s via the standard pipeline formula (15ms + 999,999×10ms), with the 33% speedup computed correctly; Goes three levels deep — mechanics, steady-state formula, latency vs. throughput distinction, and second-order bottleneck implications; Notes the numerical negligibility of fill overhead ('15ms out of 10,000s') showing some process awareness, but does not name potential reasoning pitfalls or explicitly question its own framing; Rechecks arithmetic and confirms answer against the question, but does not re-derive from a different angle or test boundary conditions; Acknowledges the fill-time term is negligible and distinguishes throughput from per-record latency, but does not state confidence levels or identify what assumptions would change the conclusion; Evaluates all four options with specific, mechanistic reasons for rejection (e.g., why B's 5ms claim is impossible given Stage 2 alone takes 10ms); Every conclusion traces to an explicit formula derivation with intermediate values shown — a third party could fully reproduce the reasoning chain.
